title: Today's Tech Brief: AI Crooks and Security Shake-Ups
summary: A look at Commvault's call to rethink resiliency and Trump's AI executive order
imageAlt: Image from source article for Commvault says it's time to rethink resiliency as AI crooks leave victims in a 'dark, dead' state
imageCaption: Commvault says it's time to rethink resiliency as AI crooks leave victims in a 'dark, dead' state
imageSourceTitle: Commvault says it's time to rethink resiliency as AI crooks leave victims in a 'dark, dead' state

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your tech brief. Today, I'm looking at some significant developments in the world of technology, particularly in security and AI regulation. It's a lot to unpack, so let's dive right in.

First up, Commvault is sounding the alarm on our current approach to resiliency in the face of AI-powered cyber attacks. According to them, these attacks are leaving victims in a 'dark, dead' state, and our current backup plans just aren't cutting it. The crux of the issue seems to be that these plans need backup testing - a step that's often overlooked until it's too late. It's a bit like having a fire extinguisher that's never been checked to see if it actually works.

Next, Trump's executive order on AI models has hit a roadblock. The plan aims to test AI models for potential dangers, but there's a problem: US security teams have been significantly gutted, making it challenging to carry out these tests effectively. It's a bit like trying to put out a fire without a fire department. And to make matters more complicated, the people who are still working in these teams are probably feeling a bit overworked and underappreciated.

Commvault says it's time to rethink resiliency as AI crooks leave victims in a 'dark, dead' state is also worth a quick mention. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Trump plan to test AI models has a problem—US security teams were gutted by DOGE is also worth a quick mention. My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
